decks.notes: This Dimir Control deck focuses on disrupting the opponent's game plan by using a combination of hand disruption, removal spells, and graveyard manipulation. By gradually wearing down the opponent's resources and limiting their options, this deck aims to gain control of the game and ultimately outvalue the opponent with powerful finishers like Sheoldred, the Apocalypse. The deck also includes ways to generate card advantage, such as Castle Locthwain and Necrodominance, to ensure that the player maintains a steady stream of threats and answers throughout the game.

The deck focuses on assembling the Allosaurus Combo, utilizing cards like Neoform and Eldritch Evolution to quickly search for and put into play key pieces like Griselbrand and Atraxa, Grand Unifier. By using creatures like Allosaurus Rider and Ghalta, Stampede Tyrant to generate mana, the deck can cast powerful spells like Pact of Negation to protect its combo pieces. The deck also includes disruption and protection in cards like Veil of Summer and Repudiate/Replicate. Ultimately, the goal is to quickly assemble the combo and overwhelm the opponent before they can mount a defense.

The deck focuses on utilizing artifacts like Mox Opal and Urza's Saga to quickly create a board presence and overwhelm the opponent with aggressive creatures like Kappa Cannoneer and Arcbound Ravager. Engineered Explosives and Tormod's Crypt help control the board while Emry, Lurker of the Loch provides recursion for key artifacts. The deck also includes disruption with cards like Metallic Rebuke and Sink into Stupor to disrupt the opponent's game plan. Overall, the deck aims to beat down the opponent quickly and efficiently using artifact synergy and aggressive creatures.

This deck focuses on using a variety of creatures with different abilities to create a powerful combo. It uses tools like Chord of Calling and Birthing Ritual to search for and cheat out creatures like Cauldron Familiar and Gilded Goose. Malevolent Rumble and Badgermole Cub provide additional synergy within the deck. The deck also includes Viscera Seer and Carrion Feeder for sacrificing creatures and generating value. The toolbox approach allows for flexibility in responding to different situations and assembling the optimal combo for victory.

This deck focuses on aggressive plays with a mix of creatures, spells, and lands that support a fast-paced strategy. It utilizes powerful creatures like Ragavan, Nimble Pilferer and Scion of Draco, alongside removal spells like Lightning Bolt and Leyline Binding to control the board. The deck also includes cards like Stubborn Denial and Teferi, Time Raveler for disruption and protection. The mana base is optimized for consistency and speed, with cards like Wooded Foothills and Arid Mesa to fetch the necessary colors. Overall, the deck aims to overwhelm the opponent with early pressure and efficient threats.

The deck focuses on quickly filling the graveyard with key creatures such as Griselbrand and Atraxa, Grand Unifier, using cards like Faithful Mending and Quantum Riddler. It then utilizes cards like Goryo's Vengeance and Ephemerate to bring these creatures back onto the battlefield for powerful combos and instant reanimation. Thoughtseize and Force of Negation help protect the combo pieces, while Prismatic Ending and Fatal Push provide removal options. The deck's strength lies in its ability to quickly assemble its combo pieces and overwhelm the opponent with powerful instant reanimation plays.

The deck focuses on utilizing alternative resources and spells to quickly assemble a powerful combo without relying on traditional lands. By utilizing cards like Disrupting Shoal and Spell Snare to disrupt the opponent's game plan, while also ramping up with Lotus Bloom and Goblin Charbelcher for a quick win condition. The deck also utilizes cards like Sea Gate Restoration and Sink into Stupor for card advantage and disruption. The key strategy is to quickly assemble the combo pieces and disrupt the opponent's game plan while remaining flexible and adaptable with various spells and artifacts.

The deck focuses on utilizing fast and efficient spells and creatures to overwhelm the opponent quickly. It includes cards like Lightning Bolt and Lava Dart for direct damage, Monastery Swiftspear for early aggression, and Slickshot Show-Off for additional damage potential. The deck also has card draw and filtering with Expressive Iteration and Mishra's Bauble to help maintain a consistent flow of threats. Overall, the deck aims to apply pressure from multiple angles and close out the game before the opponent has a chance to stabilize.

The deck focuses on utilizing various spells and abilities to ramp up mana production and quickly assemble the Goblin Charbelcher win condition. By carefully managing disruption and control spells, the deck aims to protect the combo pieces while slowing down the opponent's game plan. The key strategy is to efficiently search for and play key pieces such as Lotus Bloom, Tameshi, Reality Architect, and Thundertrap Trainer to generate the necessary resources to activate Goblin Charbelcher for a game-winning blow.

This deck centers around quickly reanimating powerful creatures and gaining advantage with a variety of instant spells. By using cards like Goryo's Vengeance and Ephemerate, you can bring back creatures like Griselbrand and Atraxa, Grand Unifier to overwhelm your opponent. Additionally, cards like Solitude and Prismatic Ending provide removal options to control the board. The deck aims to set up fast, explosive plays while disrupting your opponent's strategy, ultimately leading to a dominant board position and securing victory.

This UR Aggro deck focuses on quickly casting low-cost spells and aggressive creatures to deal damage to the opponent as fast as possible. By utilizing cards like Lava Dart, Lightning Bolt, and Monastery Swiftspear, the deck aims to control the board and pressure the opponent early on. The inclusion of card draw and manipulation spells like Expressive Iteration and Preordain helps maintain card advantage and consistency in order to maintain the aggressive tempo of the deck. The deck also includes some protection and disruption elements like Mutagenic Growth and Surgical Extraction to further push through damage and disrupt the opponent's game plan.

The deck focuses on playing aggressively with low-cost spells and creatures in order to quickly deal damage to the opponent. It utilizes cards that can deal direct damage, pump up the creatures' power and toughness, and generate card advantage. The deck aims to overwhelm the opponent with a fast and efficient strategy, using a mix of burn spells and prowess creatures to control the board and pressure the opponent. It also includes land destruction spells to disrupt the opponent's strategy and maintain tempo advantage.

The main concept of this deck is to create a powerful combo using cards like Basking Broodscale, Glaring Fleshraker, and Kozilek's Command to generate an advantage on the battlefield. The deck also utilizes Eldrazi Temple and Urza's Saga to ramp up and bring out big threats like Emrakul, the Promised End. Boseiju, Who Endures helps protect key spells while Blade of the Bloodchief and Malevolent Rumble provide additional support and removal. The deck focuses on creating a strong board presence and overwhelming the opponent with a combination of creatures and spells.

The deck revolves around cycling creatures and spells to fill up the graveyard and eventually cast Living End, a powerful sorcery that brings all creatures back from the graveyard to the battlefield. The deck also utilizes force of negation to protect key spells and disrupt opponents' strategies. It includes a variety of creatures with cycling abilities to draw cards and enable the combo, as well as other disruptive creatures and spells to control the board. The deck aims to overwhelm opponents with a sudden influx of creatures and take control of the game.

The key concept of this UR Aggro deck is to overwhelm the opponent with a fast and aggressive creature-based strategy. The deck includes efficient creatures like Cori-Steel Cutter, Dragon's Rage Channeler, and Monastery Swiftspear that can quickly deal damage to the opponent. The deck also includes burn spells like Lightning Bolt and Lava Dart to finish off the opponent or clear the way for the creatures to attack. By utilizing low-cost threats and burn spells, this deck aims to quickly reduce the opponent's life total and win the game before they can stabilize.

This deck focuses on fast and aggressive plays, aiming to quickly overwhelm the opponent with efficient creatures and burn spells. The key strategy is to establish a strong early board presence with cards like Seasoned Pyromancer and Ragavan, Nimble Pilferer, while maintaining pressure with Ajani, Nacatl Pariah and Phlage, Titan of Fire's Fury. The deck also includes disruptive elements like Goblin Bombardment and Thraben Charm to control the board and clear a path for the aggressive creatures to deliver the final blow. With a mix of creatures, burn spells, and disruption, this Boros Aggro deck aims to swiftly defeat opponents in Modern 1 vs 1 matches.
